Question: Considering four peatland research locations—(1) Zoige (Ruoergai) Plateau marsh monitoring station (China) on a tussock fen microfacet with Carex-dominated minerotrophic inputs, monitored during the 2016 continuous eddy-covariance and biweekly chamber campaign and dominated by acetoclastic methanogenesis in an anoxic fen matrix with high labile C; (2) Mer Bleue Bog Research Site (Canada) on a flush fen margin with intermittent minerotrophic seepage and bryophyte fringe, surveyed during the 2015 baseline survey with a single-season chamber ensemble and dominated by acetoclastic methanogenesis in an anoxic fen matrix with high labile C; (3) Okefenokee NWR experimental plot (USA - Georgia) on a transitional lagg fen with vascular graminoid mosaics, monitored during the 2022 continuous chamber network with fortnightly spot-checks and dominated by porewater-mediated lateral DOC export with indirect CO2 flux implications; and (4) Lauma mire observatory (Latvia) on a collapsed bog pool basin with emergent aerenchymatous vegetation, observed during the 2020 pandemic-modified reduced field visits intensive summer campaign and dominated by plant-mediated methane transport via aerenchyma and stemflow—what was the smallest greenhouse gas emission rate recorded among these specified site conditions?

Question: What is the maximum organic carbon flux (grams C per square meter per year) among these four specific site records: (1) Tana River delta mangal transect 04 - Kipini reach: polyhaline tidal flat influenced by seasonal storm surge overwash; Avicennia-dominated mangrove belt with abundant pneumatophores and prop-roots; heterogeneous redox mosaic with micro-oxic rhizosphere pockets and pervasive sulfidic matrix; (2) Murray estuary saltflat transect 07 - Coorong inlet: saltpan micro-topography with evaporative hypersalinity in dry season; Saltwort-succulent fringe with sparse low-profile halophyte canopy; carbonate-cemented layer atop reducing organogenic substrate; (3) Tana River delta mangal transect 04 - Kipini reach: subtidal shallow channel margin dominated by persistent submergence; Rhizophora mucronata closed-canopy mangrove with complex prop-root network; redoximorphic transition horizon with alternating Fe(III)/Fe(II) mottles; (4) San Francisco Bay tidal marsh transect 09 - Suisun reach: mid-intertidal cordgrass meadow with daily tidal exchange and pore flushing; Scirpus-marsh mosaic with patchy emergent clumps and seasonal inundation; severely anoxic mucky peat with methanogenic signature and low redox potential?

Question: Considering the sediment cores taken from specific marsh locations characterized by human-influenced estuarine fringe collected in 2021, a managed reclaimed marsh sampled in 2016, a low marsh with dense rhizome mats sampled in 2017, and a brackish island marsh by creek banks collected in 2014, what is the smallest measured carbon amount found among these samples exhibiting transformations through microbial carbonate precipitation, redox layering due to fresh water intrusion, and methanogenic breakdown processes?
SQL Query: SELECT MIN(CarbonDensity) AS CarbonDensity FROM (SELECT CarbonDensity AS CarbonDensity FROM EstuarineSedimentCores WHERE CoreID = "CR-MP-004 (panne margin core exhibiting desiccation features)" AND MarshFacies = "urbanized estuarine fringe showing anthropogenic fill and truncated stratigraphy" AND DiageneticRegime = "carbonate precipitation influenced microbially mediated alkalinity and porewater buffering" AND SamplingEpoch = "2021" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonDensity AS CarbonDensity FROM EstuarineSedimentCores WHERE CoreID = "CR-TR-001 (Lower estuary core with intact surface peat)" AND MarshFacies = "diked reclaimed marsh with managed drainage, compaction and oxidized upper horizon" AND DiageneticRegime = "meteoric water intrusion creating persistent redoxcline stratification" AND SamplingEpoch = "2016" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonDensity AS CarbonDensity FROM EstuarineSedimentCores WHERE CoreID = "CR-MP-004 (panne margin core exhibiting desiccation features)" AND MarshFacies = "low marsh with continuous rhizome mat and frequent inundation-driven sedimentation" AND DiageneticRegime = "methanogenic terminal diagenesis with acetate-utilizing methanogens" AND SamplingEpoch = "2017" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonDensity AS CarbonDensity FROM EstuarineSedimentCores WHERE CoreID = "CR-IS-009 (island marsh core with brackish influence)" AND MarshFacies = "creek-bank erosional facies with exposed root mass and coarse detritus" AND DiageneticRegime = "carbonate precipitation influenced microbially mediated alkalinity and porewater buffering" AND SamplingEpoch = "2014");
